_notes.purpose: EC2 Spot CPU heavy job def for J06 PDF extraction (Textract integration + heavy CPU/memory). Bigger memory footprint (30 GiB) vs Fargate jpcite-crawl-heavy (32 GiB) but EC2-native to leverage Spot CE jpcite-credit-ec2-spot-cpu (512 vCPU cap, SPOT_CAPACITY_OPTIMIZED, allocation 'optimal').
_notes.submit_example: aws batch submit-job --job-name jpcite-j06-ec2-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ) --job-queue jpcite-credit-ec2-spot-cpu-queue --job-definition jpcite-crawl-ec2-cpu --container-overrides '{"environment":[{"name":"JOB_MANIFEST","value":"J06_deep_ministry_pdf_full"}]}' --profile bookyou-recovery --region ap-northeast-1
_notes.memory_note: 30720 MiB (30 GiB) — sized for r5.xlarge / m5.2xlarge class instances on Spot. Pure 32768 (32 GiB) would force m5.2xlarge (32 GiB ec2 memory minus ECS reserve ~2 GiB = unallocatable on Spot). 30720 fits comfortably under 32 GiB instance memory minus daemon overhead.
_notes.interruption_note: EC2 Spot may be reclaimed mid-job (2-min termination notice). Retry strategy retries on Spot interruption reason; J06 sweep is idempotent at the URL level (object_manifest dedupe), so partial progress is safe.
_notes.provisioning_latency_note: Spot CE provisioning of a fresh instance is typically 2-5 min vs Fargate <1 min. Warm pool reuse can drop to 30s on subsequent submissions.
